content:
- Verified and revised the existing plan for bug-ha-e2e-false-pass-via-best-effort-polling-and-timestamp-fallback; changed plan to avoid outer timeouts (spawn_local detach risk) and added removal of time=0 sentinels.
- Implemented fail-closed HA evidence checks in src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs:
  - assert_no_dual_primary_window now tracks successful full-cluster samples and errors if none succeeded.
  - assert_no_dual_primary_in_samples now errors when sample_count==0.
- Fixed fencing cutoff false-pass:
  - Removed unix_now()->0 sentinel in SQL workload commit timestamp capture; track commit_timestamp_capture_failures and fail closed when timestamps are incomplete.
  - Added strict helper count_commits_after_cutoff_strict used by the fencing e2e.
- Renamed wait_for_all_failsafe -> wait_for_no_primary_and_any_failsafe_best_effort and fixed misleading 'all nodes' wording in the matrix scenario log.
- Updated ClusterFixture::record to avoid [0] timestamps on unix_now errors (uses time_error marker instead).
- Added unit tests for the new fail-closed guards and cutoff counting.
- Validation gates all passing:
  - make check OK
  - make test OK
  - make test-long OK
  - make lint OK (noted clippy::manual_unwrap_or prefers unwrap_or over manual match).
- Marked task <passes>true</passes>, ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed + pushed. Added a learning to AGENTS.md about clippy manual_unwrap_or.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and searched for `<passes>false</passes>` / `<passes>meta-task</passes>`.
- Found two failing bug tasks; selected the higher-risk HA safety false-pass bug: `.ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md`.
- Wrote selected task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> .ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md.
- Inspected current code: `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` already appears to have the fail-closed fix (no sentinel 0 timestamp; `commit_timestamp_capture_failures`; strict cutoff counter + unit tests).
- Spawned parallel explorer agents to confirm call sites, cutoff logic, and test coverage; they all reported the code is already fail-closed and has regression tests.
- Wrote a full execution plan into `.ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md` and appended lifecycle marker `TO BE VERIFIED` per workflow.
- Next: reopen task file, do skeptical verification (must change at least one plan item), then promote to `NOW EXECUTE` and proceed to evidence + required gates + task closeout if still applicable.

- Searched HA e2e Rust tests for commit-timestamp fields and cutoff-related assertions.
- Confirmed timestamp capture path and assertion path are localized to `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`.
- Identified cutoff helper `ClusterFixture::count_commits_after_cutoff_strict` and its real use site in `e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity`.
- Reviewed unit tests already covering fail-closed behavior around timestamp capture completeness, zero timestamps, and strict > cutoff semantics.
- No code changes made in this pass.

- User asked for Cloudflare Pages auto-build command for mdBook and whether mdBook is installed.
- Read progress log (empty output), inspected repo for wrangler config and docs scripts.
- Found no wrangler config in repo; found tools/install-mdbook.sh (downloads pinned mdBook v0.4.40 binary) and tools/install-mdbook-mermaid.sh (requires cargo, installs v0.14.0).
- Verified CF docs: Git integration auto deploys on commit; build command is configured in Pages settings; direct upload via Wrangler is different path.
- Verified CF build-image docs: v3 language/tool list does not include Rust/cargo by default, so mdbook-mermaid cargo install likely fails unless Rust toolchain is installed in build command.
- Next: provide exact Build command for Pages that bootstraps Rust only when missing, runs existing install scripts, and builds docs to docs/book; also provide minimal wrangler.jsonc guidance where applicable.

- Reviewed bug plan file `.ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md` and compared it against current code.
- Found the plan is partially stale: `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` already has fail-closed timestamp capture and strict cutoff checks + unit tests.
- Found an uncovered sentinel fallback still present in `src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs` (`Err(_) => 0` in timeline record), which the plan scope currently misses.
- Next: provide user with weaknesses and concrete robust changes: tighten step ordering, broaden sentinel audit scope, make monotonic hardening non-optional, and require deterministic targeted tests/evidence gates with no optionality.

- Investigated whether commit timestamp fallback-to-0 bug still exists, starting with src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs and related HA harness modules.
- Searched for unix_now, unwrap_or(0), Err(_) => 0, and timestamp handling. In e2e_multi_node, commit timestamp capture now uses match on unix_now and records failure counters instead of writing 0; strict cutoff validation rejects zero timestamps and incomplete capture; tests cover zero-timestamp rejection.
- Found remaining fallback-to-0 in related modules: src/test_harness/ha_e2e/ops.rs record() uses Err(_) => 0 for timeline stamps; src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs record() does same; src/test_harness/ports.rs unlease_ports_best_effort uses unix_now_ms().unwrap_or(0). These appear non-commit-timestamp pathways but are similar patterns.
- Next if asked: patch related fallbacks to avoid silent 0 defaults and propagate/annotate timing errors consistently.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md`.
- Detected task file was in `TO BE VERIFIED` state; performed deep skeptical verification using parallel subagents.
- Confirmed the original bug (commit timestamp fallback to 0 in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`) is already fixed: commit timestamp capture fails closed via `commit_timestamp_capture_failures`, strict cutoff helper rejects incomplete/0 timestamps, and unit tests exist.
- Found remaining `Err(_) => 0` / `unwrap_or(0)` sentinel time fallbacks elsewhere (`src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs`, `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/ops.rs`, `src/test_harness/ports.rs`) and updated plan to require a repo-wide audit so adjacent sentinel time patterns can't silently persist.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md` plan: removed optional monotonic hardening, made `make test-long` mandatory, added explicit audit step, and promoted marker to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: execute the plan exactly as written (no more exploration): create evidence dir, run all 4 required gates with logs captured, then close out task + task_switch + commit + push.

- User asked to mirror OG docs publish flow but switch to mdBook output and target pgtuskmaster-docs dist/out.
- Read prior progress log, inspected ../pgtuskmaster/scripts/publish-docs.sh and mapped its behavior (bootstrap docs repo, token sourcing, remote sync, copy, commit, push).
- Added new script at scripts/publish-docs.sh in pgtuskmaster_rust.
- Script now ensures mdBook + mdbook-mermaid installers run when missing, builds docs via mdbook build docs, copies docs/book -> ../pgtuskmaster-docs/dist/out, commits and pushes to main with tokenized remote.
- Wrangler bootstrap in script now defaults assets.directory to ./dist/out when missing.
- Validated script syntax with bash -n.
- Next: if user wants, run script end-to-end once to verify push path and adjust wrangler behavior if they prefer forcing update even when wrangler already exists.
